message:
fix(wish): prevent split escape sequences in SSH handler

Applies the same is_sequence_prefix() check from bubbletea to the wish
SSH handler's input processing loop. When a partial ANSI escape sequence
is detected (e.g., "\x1b[" without the terminating character), the
handler now breaks from the loop to wait for more data rather than
attempting to parse incomplete sequences.

This fixes race conditions where fast typing or network latency could
cause escape sequences to arrive split across multiple SSH data packets,
resulting in spurious Escape key events or garbled input.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
